summary: The patient’s symptoms—rash, hypotension, and dyspnea—are indicative of anaphylaxis after ampicillin infusion. Initial management involves immediate intramuscular epinephrine at 0.3–0.5 mL of 1:1000 solution as first-line therapy because it provides fast action with lower cardiac risks than other routes; intravenous epinephrine is only used if there are signs of persistent shock or inadequate response to IM dosing.
